title: Provide a base Docker image with the packages already installed
close_reason: Base Docker images configured for Docker Hub distribution at rootlyio/on-call-health-*-base

comments.author: Sylvain Kalache
comments.text:
Implementation complete. Created:
- backend/Dockerfile.base: Base image with Python packages pre-installed
- frontend/Dockerfile.base: Base image with node_modules pre-installed  
- scripts/build-base-images.sh: Script to build base images
- Updated backend/Dockerfile, frontend/Dockerfile, frontend/Dockerfile.dev to use base images via ARG
- Updated docker-compose.yml to pass BASE_IMAGE build args

Usage: Run ./scripts/build-base-images.sh once when dependencies change, then docker-compose up builds much faster.

comments.author: Sylvain Kalache
comments.text:
Updated to use Docker Hub registry (rootlyio namespace):
- Base images: rootlyio/on-call-health-backend-base, rootlyio/on-call-health-frontend-base
- Build script now supports --push flag to push to Docker Hub
- All Dockerfiles default to pulling from Docker Hub

title: Show percentage contribution of each item in Risk Analysis tile
description: Display the percentage that each individual item in the Risk Analysis tile contributes to the total risk score
close_reason: Implemented percentage contribution display for Risk Analysis tile

comments.author: Sylvain Kalache
comments.text: Review the way that the contributing risks are displayed, and rank them by order of contribution. Prior, we were displaying a score with digits, now we should show the % it contributed to. For example, let's say the user has a risk level score of 20, 5 points for outside of work hour (would be 25%), 15 points for number of incidents (would be 75%).

comments.author: Sylvain Kalache
comments.text: Review the data is presented, bullet point ain't gonna make it

comments.author: Sylvain Kalache
comments.text:
Implementation complete:

Backend (app/core/ocb_config.py):
- Added get_structured_ocb_factors() function that extracts factor data with percentages

Backend (app/services/unified_burnout_analyzer.py):
- Added ocb_factors field to API response containing structured factor data sorted by contribution

Frontend (MemberDetailModal.tsx):
- Redesigned Contributing Factors section to use new structured data
- Factors now sorted by percentage contribution (highest first)
- Shows percentage instead of points (e.g., '25%' instead of '5.2 points')
- Replaced bullet points with progress bars color-coded by dimension (indigo=Personal, violet=Work-Related)
- Added fallback to old display for backwards compatibility
